queue.task: Session B kickoff: review Session A output, prioritize Normal TODO items for depth work
queue.summary: Session B kickoff. Queue is depth-focused: VM closure mutation, type affinity, escape analysis, MEDIAN. No queue changes.

queue.goal: Fix monkey-lang VM closure mutation (evaluator returns 3, VM returns 0)
queue.summary: VM closure mutation already fixed by Session A const-subst removeMutated fix! All 4 mutation patterns match (counter, shared state, nested, accumulator). Filling BUILD with regression tests.

queue.task: Verify VM closure mutation is fixed — write regression tests
queue.summary: 9 closure mutation regression tests: counter, shared state, nested, accumulator, decrement, toggle, independent, loop, string. All pass eval+VM. Bug was already fixed.

queue.task: placeholder
Housekeeping: git push, dashboard health
queue.summary: Git pushed workspace + monkey-lang. Located henrydb at projects/henrydb/.

queue.task: Reflect on VM closure fix. Pick next depth target.
queue.summary: VM closure was already fixed. Queue order good: type affinity → escape analysis. No changes.

queue.goal: HenryDB: close fuzzer type affinity gap (TEXT >= INT coercion in WHERE)
queue.summary: Root cause: HenryDB stores int 42 in TEXT column as int, SQLite coerces to string. Fix: add applyAffinity() in insert-row.js after value resolution. SQLite affinity rules: TEXT→string, INT→tryNumber, REAL→tryFloat, NUMERIC→flexible.

queue.task: Add SQLite type affinity on INSERT — coerce values based on column type declarations
queue.summary: Created type-affinity.js shared module. INSERT now coerces values via applyAffinity(). ORDER BY uses sqliteCompare(). All existing tests pass.

queue.task: Run fuzzer across 10 seeds to measure improvement
queue.summary: Fuzzer results across 10 seeds (200 iters each): avg 96.5%. Best 99.5%, worst 94%. Remaining failures: window function ORDER BY, UNION type issues. Also fixed fuzzer match variable bug.

queue.task: placeholder
queue.summary: Skipped — placeholder, actual BUILD work done in T22/T23.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, knowledge capture

queue.goal: Explore lambda-calculus project — 43K lines, 190 PL theory modules

queue.task: Deep dive into henrydb parameter binding ( params)
queue.summary: Parameter binding explored. PREPARE/EXECUTE works. Programmatic prepare().execute([params]) works. Missing: db.execute(sql, [params]) convenience method. Fix: parse SQL, bindParams, then execute_ast.

queue.task: Mid-session: assess progress, plan next depth cycle
queue.summary: 13 min in, real work: type affinity module, 9 regression tests, fuzzer at 96.5%. Next: escape analysis (deepest), then MEDIAN, then param binding fix.

queue.goal: monkey-lang: wire escape analysis into stack-allocated closures
queue.summary: Plan: (1) Run escape analysis during compilation, annotate CompiledFunction with escapesScope flag, (2) VM skips _track for non-escaping closures, (3) Test coverage. Key insight: JS VM cannot do real stack alloc, but can skip GC tracking.

queue.task: Wire escape analysis into compiler: annotate CompiledFunction with escape info
queue.summary: Wired escape analysis into compiler. Fixed 2 escape analysis bugs (anonymous FunctionLiteral handling, implicit return detection). CompiledFunction now has escapes field. All tests pass.

queue.task: Add non-escaping closure optimization: skip GC tracking for stack-local closures
queue.summary: VM now skips _track() for non-escaping closures. Optimization wired end-to-end: escape analysis → compiler annotation → VM optimization. All tests pass.

queue.task: Add escape analysis tests for compiler integration
queue.summary: 13 escape analysis integration tests: 4 detection, 3 compiler annotations, 6 VM correctness. All pass. Complete end-to-end pipeline verified.
